findings.title: N=0 Agent Count Produces Silent False-Proceed Through Convergence Gate
findings.description: When Phase 2 fails completely (all agents timeout/error), findings-helper.sh convergence emits 0.0\t0\t0\t0. The formula effective_threshold = 0.6 * (0/5) = 0.0. The gate condition overlap_ratio (0.0) > 0.0 is false, so execution proceeds to Step 2.5.1 with zero agents. Synthesis receives empty reaction directory with no error signal. The interspect event stores agents_dispatched: 0 identically to a legitimate full-convergence skip.
findings.fix: Add explicit N=0 guard at Step 2.5.0: If agent_count==0, skip with error event type (reaction-skipped.json + interspect-reaction event with {type:error, reason:no_phase2_agents}). Do not proceed.
findings.security_impact: Operator cannot distinguish silent Phase 2 failure from legitimate full-convergence skip. Post-hoc audit is unreliable.

findings.title: Peer-Priming Discount Applied After Overlap Ratio Computed, Creating Double-Discount Risk
findings.description: Spec says: discount findings from overlap count BEFORE computing overlap_ratio. But findings-helper.sh convergence already computes the ratio. Spec then says run script first, then apply discount. This creates ambiguity: Interpretation A (correct) recomputes ratio after discount. Interpretation B (wrong) subtracts discount fraction from already-computed ratio. An LLM orchestrator following the prose literally applies B. Additionally, if all findings are peer-primed, count goes negative (underflow), producing negative ratio that always passes gate.
findings.fix: Rewrite peer-priming paragraph: Compute discounted_overlapping = max(0, overlapping_findings - peer_primed_count), then overlap_ratio = discounted_overlapping / max(1, total_findings). Replace script's output ratio with adjusted value.
findings.security_impact: Convergence gate can fire/not-fire non-deterministically based on implementation interpretation.

findings.title: Completion-Signaling Contract Not Applied to Reaction Agents
findings.description: Phase 2 establishes robust completion contract: agents write .md.partial, rename to .md, orchestrator monitors via flux-watch.sh, retries once on partial-only, writes error stubs on second failure. Phase 2.5 dispatch specifies run_in_background: true and 60s timeout but: no monitoring equivalent to flux-watch, no .partial sentinel, no retry-once, no collection logic. Synthesis detects completion by checking for .reactions.md files, so partial completion goes undetected.
findings.fix: Add Step 2.5.4a: Monitor reaction agents via flux-watch.sh {OUTPUT_DIR} {N} {timeout_ms} using pattern *.reactions.md. Collect after timeout. Apply partial-file retry-once pattern from Phase 2.

findings.title: Peer Findings Injected Into Reaction Prompts Without Sanitization
findings.description: {peer_findings} content assembled from Findings Index blocks via findings-helper.sh, injected directly into reaction-prompt.md with no sanitization. _interspect_sanitize() function (injection-rejection at lib-interspect.sh:2725-2731) is wired only to DB insertion, not prompt assembly. A compromised/adversarial flux-gen agent can embed instruction-like text in Findings Index title, which reaches LLM prompt. Trust boundary violation: AGENTS.md declares flux-gen agents as untrusted inputs.
findings.fix: Apply _interspect_sanitize() to each Findings Index line before inserting into {peer_findings}. Apply same sanitization to {agent_name}, {agent_description}, {own_findings_index}.
findings.security_impact: Prompt injection vulnerability in multi-agent prompt assembly. Flux-gen agent outputs can manipulate reaction agent behavior.

findings.title: Peer-Priming Discount Requires Timestamp Ordering Not Implemented in findings-helper.sh
findings.description: Peer-priming discount requires comparing timestamps: peer-findings.jsonl timestamp < Findings Index entry timestamp. But findings-helper.sh read-indexes extracts Findings Index lines with no timestamp field (awk block at lines 74-81 extracts only severity/ID/section/title). Findings Index markdown format has no per-entry timestamps. Comparison is unexecutable as specified.
findings.fix: Define peer-findings.jsonl schema (minimum: title, agent, timestamp). Specify that agent .md file mtime serves as Findings Index entry timestamp, comparable to peer-findings JSONL entry timestamp via stat -c %Y.

findings.title: {agent_description} Template Variable Has No Resolution Path
findings.description: reaction-prompt.md uses {agent_description} in agent identity header: You are **{agent_name}** ({agent_description}). Step 2.5.3-4 lists filled variables: {agent_name}, {own_findings_index}, {peer_findings}, {fixative_context}, {output_path}. No {agent_description}. Implementer produces reaction prompt with literal {agent_description} placeholder or causes substitution error.
findings.fix: Add to fill list at Step 2.5.3-4: Read {agent_description} from agent .md file frontmatter (first non-example sentence), fall back to role description from agent-roles.yaml. Or remove {agent_description} from prompt template if agent self-identification via {agent_name} alone is sufficient.

findings.title: mode_overrides Applied in Config But Not Checked in Spec Prose
findings.description: reaction.yaml defines mode_overrides: {quality-gates: false, review: true, flux-drive: true}. Spec only tests reaction_round.enabled. Never reads mode_overrides[MODE]. Orchestrator in quality-gates mode executes reaction round when it should skip.
findings.fix: Add MODE guard at top of Step 2.5.0 before convergence gate: If mode_overrides[MODE] == false, skip to Phase 3 immediately.

findings.title: Skip-Path Emits Wrong Event Type Label
findings.description: Step 2.5.0 skip branch says: emit interspect-reaction event via _interspect_emit_reaction_dispatched(). String 'interspect-reaction' is the review_type tag passed to _interspect_insert_evidence inside that function, not the event type. Event type stored is 'reaction_dispatched'. Calling this interspect-reaction creates terminology mismatch.
findings.fix: Spec should say: emit a reaction_dispatched evidence record (type: skip) via _interspect_emit_reaction_dispatched().

findings.title: Peer-Priming Discount Timestamp Source Unspecified
findings.description: Instruction: check if first report timestamp in peer-findings.jsonl precedes Findings Index entry timestamp. Findings Index is static with no per-entry timestamps. Instruction implies comparing JSONL timestamp against Findings Index timestamp, but Findings Index format contains no timestamp field.
findings.fix: Clarify: A finding is peer-primed if it appears in peer-findings.jsonl AND the JSONL entry timestamp precedes the agent's .md file modification time (stat -c %Y).

findings.title: {agent_description} Template Variable Undefined in Orchestrator Spec
findings.description: Same as ARCH-05 — template variable appears in prompt but not in fill list.

findings.title: mode_overrides Config Key Consumed Nowhere in Reaction Spec
Peer-Priming Discount Is Fully Agent-Instruction-Dependent With No Verification
findings.description: peer-findings.jsonl written by Phase 2 agents with no signature/write-once enforcement. Agent can back-date or forward-date entries to suppress/promote peer-priming. Discount affects overlap_ratio (go/no-go gate), so this impacts pipeline correctness.
findings.fix: Record peer-findings.jsonl entries with server-issued monotonic timestamp (not agent-provided), or cross-validate against agent output file mtime.

findings.title: Convergence Gate Title Normalization Is Bypassable by Minor Rewording
findings.description: findings-helper.sh convergence normalizes by stripping non-alphanumeric and lowercasing. Agent can introduce minimal variation: 'unbounded memory growth' vs 'unbounded memory allocation' — different keys, not counted as overlapping. Natural language variation causes false-negatives in overlap detection, degrading convergence signal.
findings.fix: Use fuzzy matching (Jaro-Winkler or trigram, ~0.75 threshold) or move convergence detection to synthesis (post-hoc semantic dedup by synthesis agent).

findings.title: N=1 Agent Sends Zero Agents to React But Still Emits agents_dispatched: 0 Without Clear Skip vs Proceed Branch
findings.description: With agent_count=1, convergence gate does not skip (threshold 0.12, ratio 0.0 not above). But Step 2.5.2a topology leaves agent with no visible peers. Step 2.5.3 skips agents with empty peers. Result: zero dispatches with no explicit guard. Event shows agents_dispatched: 0 indistinguishably from N=0 total failure.
findings.fix: Add guard after convergence gate: If agent_count < 2, skip with {type:skip, reason:insufficient_agents}.

findings.title: awk Regex Strips Hyphens in Finding Titles, Causing Unrelated Findings to Collide
findings.description: Normalization regex `gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9 ]/, "", title)` strips hyphens. Two findings: 'Off-by-one in loop bound' and 'Off by one in loop bound' both normalize to 'off by one in loop bound'. They merge, inflating overlapping_findings count and potentially suppressing reaction round prematurely.
findings.fix: Replace line 119: gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9 \-]/, "", title); gsub(/-/, " ", title); This normalizes hyphenated and unhyphenated variants consistently.

findings.title: Fixative Sequencing Constraint Stated But Not Enforced — Race Condition Latent
findings.description: Spec states: Step 2.5.2b MUST complete before 2.5.3 — do not parallelize. This is correct (fixative Gini needs all agents' findings) but is only prose instruction. LLM orchestrator can interpret build-prompts and compute-fixative as independent concurrent tasks. Some agents receive {fixative_context} as literal placeholder or empty string, defeating anti-echo-chamber mechanism.
findings.fix: Restructure: rename Step 2.5.2b → Compute fixative context string FC, then Step 2.5.3 say Use FC from Step 2.5.2b in each prompt. Make data dependency explicit.

findings.title: No Outer Timeout Bound for Parallel Agent Dispatch — Unbounded Worst-Case Latency
findings.description: Per-agent timeout 60s, parallel dispatch via run_in_background: true. However, runtime may queue sequentially. 12 agents × 60s = 720s worst case with no progress signal. No outer circuit-breaker if timeout enforcement itself fails.
findings.fix: Add to Step 2.5.5: If total elapsed time exceeds 2 * timeout_seconds, treat remaining in-flight agents as timed out. Do not block indefinitely.

findings.title: session_id Not Acquired Before Interspect Emission Call
findings.description: _interspect_emit_reaction_dispatched() requires session_id=$1 with enforcement. Spec never specifies how to acquire it. Skip-path at Step 2.5.0 (early exit before library sourced) has same gap. Implementer produces call that fails with 'session_id required'.
findings.fix: Add callout before first emission: Acquire session_id: source lib-interspect.sh && session_id=$(_interspect_session_id) or use $CLAUDE_SESSION_ID if set.

findings.title: Peer-Priming Timestamp Comparison Has No Defined Format Contract
findings.description: Spec requires comparing timestamps from two separate files with no defined format contract. peer-findings.jsonl format is undefined. Findings Index block carries no timestamps. Implementer cannot execute this logic.
findings.fix: Define peer-findings.jsonl schema (at minimum: {title, agent, timestamp}) and document timestamp comparison mechanics.

findings.title: Scaled Threshold Cap Formula Prose Is Incorrect for N Between 5 and 8
findings.description: Spec states: For N=8, threshold caps at 0.6. Incorrect. Formula effective_threshold = min(0.6*(N/5), 0.6) caps for all N>=5. Prose misleads implementer into thinking N=6, N=7 have uncapped thresholds, causing inflated thresholds 0.72, 0.84 before capping.

findings.title: context_json Passed to Interspect Truncated at 500 Chars — Structured JSON Silently Mangled
findings.description: _interspect_insert_evidence calls _interspect_sanitize "$context_json" with default 500-char limit. _interspect_emit_reaction_dispatched assembles 10-field JSON before passing. Long input_path (deeply nested OUTPUT_DIR) or review_id causes overflow. Bash substring truncation produces invalid JSON, which becomes {} via fallback. Evidence row stored with no context, making skip event invisible.
findings.fix: Pass explicit max_chars=2000 to _interspect_sanitize when sanitizing context_json, or sanitize only string fields, leaving numerics un-truncated.

findings.title: convergence_before Event Field Records Post-Discount Value, Not Pre-Discount
findings.description: convergence_before field described as 'overlap_ratio from Step 2.5.0'. If peer-priming discount was applied, stored value is discounted ratio, not raw. Downstream consumers expecting pre-discount value underestimate natural convergence trends.

findings.title: SCT-02 Isolation Fallback Upgrades All Agents to Summary Visibility Silently
findings.description: Isolation fallback fires when zero visible peers exist (SCT-02), upgrades visibility to summary from all peers. Fires silently — no audit trail. If topology config is misconfigured (e.g., all agents mapped to role with no adjacencies), every agent silently receives fallback and topology is bypassed.

findings.title: Fixative Injection Content Concatenated Directly Into Agent Prompt Without Sanitization Gate
findings.description: Fixative config contains injection text (imbalance, convergence, drift, collapse injections) concatenated into fixative_context and passed verbatim into reaction prompt. If config modified (compromised commit, test config), adversarial injection text reaches agent prompts. Lower threat than REACT-02 (config is in project repo under source control) but spec does not mandate sanitization.

findings.title: partially-agree Maps to distinction in Move Type Table But Prompt Body Maps Same to defense
findings.description: Output format block lists Move Type enumeration: attack|defense|new-assertion|concession (no distinction). Move Type Assignment section maps partially-agree → distinction. Agent reading output format first will never use distinction, producing outputs that synthesis must reclassify. Hearsay rule depends on move type being defense with independent Evidence — if partially-agree agents emit defense, confirmations with partial evidence incorrectly escape hearsay weighting.

findings.title: Agents Skipped on Empty Peer Findings Are Silently Skipped With No Accounting
findings.description: Step 2.5.3 says: Skip agents with empty peer findings. These skipped agents not dispatched but no bucket in report format for agents_skipped. Operator cannot distinguish '3 dispatched, 0 errors' from '3 dispatched (5 skipped), 0 errors'.

findings.title: max_reactions_per_agent Config Key Unused By Spec
findings.description: reaction.yaml defines max_reactions_per_agent: 3. Spec and prompt harden code "at most 3". Config key is never read during prompt fill. If operator changes config, prompt is not updated and config becomes dead.

findings.title: Agents-With-Empty-Peer-Findings Skip Rule Produces Silent No-Op Without Event Record
findings.description: Step 2.5.3 says skip agents with empty peer findings. No instruction to record which agents skipped or why. No reactions_skipped field in evidence payload. If all agents skipped this way, Step 2.5.5 shows 0 dispatched with no explanation.

findings.title: session_id Call-Site Argument Omitted From Both Emit Call Descriptions
findings.description: Step 2.5.0 and 2.5.5 describe calling _interspect_emit_reaction_dispatched() with named arguments. Neither mention first positional argument session_id that function requires. Implementer building call site from spec alone produces failing call.

findings.title: severity_filter_p2_light Interaction With Topology Visibility Unspecified
findings.description: Step 2.5.2 filters findings to P0/P1 (optional P2 via severity_filter_p2_light). Step 2.5.2a applies topology-based visibility. Unclear whether summary visibility includes P2 findings when filter is true, or whether severity filtering happens before topology filtering.

findings.title: collapse_threshold: 2 vs 'BOTH' — Numeric vs Boolean Semantics Diverge
findings.description: YAML sets collapse_threshold: 2, comment says 'fires if imbalance AND convergence both trigger'. Spec says 'fires if both trigger'. Does threshold value mean 'count >= 2 (any two)' or 'exactly the two named triggers'? Spec does not clarify.

findings.title: Timeout Enforcement Responsibility Not Assigned (Orchestrator vs Agent Runtime)
findings.description: Step 2.5.3-4 specifies timeout_seconds for dispatched agents but does not state who enforces it or what happens on breach (kill agent? count as error? wait indefinitely?). Monitoring contract in shared-contracts.md not referenced.

findings.title: Skip-Event agent_count Field Not Cross-Validated — Inaccurate Fleet Size in Telemetry
findings.description: Skip event emits agents_dispatched: 0 and agent_count: N. agent_count from findings-helper.sh convergence (counts distinct agent names in Findings Index) equals Phase 2 completions, not dispatches. If agents failed with error stubs producing no Findings Index, agent_count undercounts. Distorts post-hoc analysis.

findings.title: Sycophancy Detection Is Per-Agent Aggregate, Not Per-Finding
findings.description: Per-agent sycophancy (high agreement_rate + low independence across all reactions) does not detect coordinated minority suppression (3 agents agree to disagree with 2 on specific finding). Config comment acknowledges per-finding sycophancy is future enhancement.

findings.title: Isolation Fallback (SCT-02) Upgrades All Agents to Summary Visibility Silently
findings.description: When agent has zero visible peers after topology filtering, fallback_on_isolation upgrades visibility to summary from all peers. Fires silently — no reaction-skipped.json, no event field recording which agents triggered fallback, no audit line in report. If topology config error (all agents mapped to role with no adjacencies), every agent silently receives fallback.

items: Write {OUTPUT_DIR}/convergence-debug.json at convergence gate with normalized title map, overlap set, effective_threshold, peer-primed discounts, final ratio
Formalize peer-findings.jsonl write protocol with --agent argument cross-check against filename convention
Add convergence-gate --dry-run mode to findings-helper.sh for testability without full orchestration
Document trust boundary for flux-gen agent outputs at code level (sanitization applied before prompt injection)
Clarify _interspect_sanitize scope: which data paths apply sanitization before LLM injection vs before DB storage
Document calibration fleet size (5) in convergence threshold formula and make it configurable if standard fleet size changes
Move convergence detection to synthesis phase (post-hoc semantic dedup) rather than pre-reaction awk script
Use fuzzy string matching (Jaro-Winkler or trigram ~0.75) for title overlap instead of exact normalized match
Add isolation_fallback_agents (count or list) to reaction-dispatched interspect event context
Implement per-finding sycophancy detection in synthesis agent (detect coordinated minority suppression)
Add reaction-round completion timeout to integration contract (e.g., max(60*agent_count, 300) seconds)
Track hearsay vs independent confirmations at per-finding granularity in synthesis evidence schema

perspectives.framing: Phase 2.5 correctness requires invariants on convergence gate, peer-priming, and fixative sequencing; N=0 violates empty-findings safety invariant; peer-priming ambiguity violates determinism. Identified edge cases (N=0, context truncation) with precision.

flux-gen agents are untrusted inputs per AGENTS.md boundary; peer-findings reach prompts unsanitized; timestamp-based discount is unverifiable; context truncation silently mangles evidence. Brings threat modeling perspective.

mode_overrides config field is dead code; move-type enumerations contradict; timeout responsibility unassigned; multiple spec prose errors create implementer guesswork. Flags specification completeness gaps.

Phase 2.5 is architecturally coherent but integration gaps exist: no completion-wait contract (differs from Phase 2 pattern), unimplemented peer-priming logic, undocumented session_id acquisition. Connects to prior work.

summary: Phase 2.5 reaction round specification is architecturally sound with well-sequenced convergence gating, topology-aware visibility, fixative injection, and parallel dispatch. However, 2 P0 correctness defects (silent N=0 false-proceed, peer-priming double-discounting ambiguity) and 12 P1 integration/security gaps (prompt injection, missing completion contract, unexecutable peer-priming algorithm, session_id undocumented) must be resolved before implementation. Most serious risks: silent convergence gate failures, unsanitized flux-gen outputs reaching LLM prompts, missing peer-findings.jsonl schema. All issues have targeted fixes; none require redesign. Synthesized discourse shows healthy diversity (Gini=0.15, novelty=1.0) and strong convergence (80% agreement on issue legitimacy).

before_implementation: Fix RXN-01: Add N=0 explicit guard at Step 2.5.0
Fix RXN-02: Rewrite peer-priming discount with explicit formula
Fix Q-04: Define peer-findings.jsonl schema or use file mtime
Fix REACT-02: Apply _interspect_sanitize to all template variables
Fix RXN-04: Update awk regex to preserve hyphens
Fix ARCH-01: Add flux-watch monitoring step after dispatch
Fix ARCH-03: Document session_id acquisition before emissions
before_production: Fix ARCH-06 + Q-01: Add MODE guard
Fix RXN-05: Make fixative sequencing explicit as data dependency
Fix REACT-08: Increase context truncation limit to 2000 chars
next_iteration: Implement fuzzy title matching (0.75 similarity threshold)
Document trust boundary for flux-gen outputs
